issues.title: Benchmark harness measuring framework success
issues.description: Build a repeatable benchmark that runs a defined reference project (spec'd in advance) through agent execution and measures whether this framework earns its overhead. Three measures, per Brooks: (1) total token consumption, (2) % of project completed to spec, (3) wall-clock time. Design should compare scaffolded (to-execution Instance) vs unscaffolded baseline runs on the same spec. Needs its own grill → PRD before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: A reference spec exists against which % completion is mechanically assessable
One command (or documented procedure) produces all three measures for a run
Scaffolded vs unscaffolded comparison is possible on the same spec
issues.notes: Filed by Team Lead from Brooks's directive 2026-06-07, mid-EXEC-001 grill.

issues.title: Verify project-scoped publish auth bypasses 2FA on the next real release
issues.description: On the next real to-execution publish, confirm the RELEASE.md recipe publishes unattended (no OTP prompt / no E403). npm-personal-token may be a classic token that 2FA-gates writes; whoami can't prove write-2FA. If it OTP/E403s, overwrite the Keychain item (service npm-personal-token, account $USER) value with an automation/granular-automation token that bypasses 2FA — src/.npmrc and the recipe stay unchanged.
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-09 as the deferred 2FA-bypass-on-write proof for EXEC-034 (Brooks).

issues.title: Ship the viewer server as a separate globally-installed package; sessions detect or connect to it
issues.description: Once EXEC-054 validates the in-repo auto-run server, extract it into its own package installed globally (separate from the to-execution Scaffolder package). Individual execution repos/sessions check whether the global server exists and connect to it instead of each running their own — one server, many Instances. Same hard requirements: lightweight, fast-running, zero/near-zero dependencies. Needs design decisions (port/discovery convention, multi-repo serving, version skew between server and Instances) — grill before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: One globally-installed server on a well-known port serves every registered repo at a stable URL
SessionStart hooks register the repo instead of spawning a per-repo server when the global server is present
Viewer assets served from the repo, not the package (version-skew policy per ADR-0007)
Idle/orphan and lockdown guarantees of EXEC-054 hold
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Step 2 of the viewer-server track; acceptance criteria set after EXEC-054 validates and a grill resolves discovery/packaging. Re-identified from EXEC-047 on 2026-06-10: the original id collided with a PRD-007 hook slice filed in a parallel session; sprint-4 close gate caught the lineage mismatch.

issues.title: Edit and add issues from the status-page viewer
issues.description: Extend the status-page viewer from read-only to read-write for issues: add new issues and edit existing ones from the web page. Writes go through the server (EXEC-054/055) since a static page cannot write the .excn JSON; every write must remain schema-valid against issue.schema.json and respect the issue partitioning (backlog vs sprint partitions, scribe ownership of moves). Hard requirements: keep it as lightweight and fast as possible — no framework, minimal surface.
acceptance_criteria: Issues can be added and edited from the status page; writes validate against issue.schema.json and land atomically
Ids are assigned by the server scanning all partitions (scribe-assigns-ids rule honored)
Schema-invalid payloads are rejected with a named error
Partition moves and lifecycle remain agent work
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Builds on the viewer-server track; interaction with scribe ownership of issue JSON to be resolved at grill time. Re-identified from EXEC-048 on 2026-06-10: the original id collided with a PRD-007 hook slice filed in a parallel session; sprint-4 close gate caught the lineage mismatch.

issues.title: Ensure Team Leads spawn persistent Teammates as persistent, not as transient subagents
issues.description: Observed (Brooks): Team Lead sessions sometimes spawn agents inappropriately — a persistent Teammate gets spawned as a transient Invoked Agent/subagent, losing membership, continuity, and messaging addressability. CONTEXT.md draws the Teammate-vs-Invoked-Agent line by lifecycle, and sprint 4 defect D4-1 already forced a persistent-Teammate-spawning rule mid-sprint, so the failure mode is real here too. Explore how to make correct spawning systematic rather than remembered: candidates include a TEAM_DIRECTIVE/agent-def instruction sweep (roster members spawn via make-teammate/teams, gates spawn via the Agent tool), a remind-only hook on the spawn tool that flags a roster-named agent being spawned transiently (rides the per-feature hook plumbing, ADR-0006), and a doctor/health check that compares the live team roster against the directive roster. Spike first: characterize when and why leads get it wrong, then pick the enforcement. Src-First.
acceptance_criteria: The failure mode is characterized: when a Team Lead spawns a roster Teammate transiently, what in the docs/tooling let it happen
A chosen mechanism (instruction, remind-only hook, or health check) makes the mismatch visible or prevented at spawn time
Spawning a roster-named Teammate persistently and an Invoked Agent transiently both pass without noise
issues.notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Related context: sprint-4 defect D4-1 fix adopted persistent-Teammate spawning for the rest of that sprint.

issues.title: Prune aged *_progress.json records
issues.description: Gitignored progress records (gate verdicts, session logs, per-PRD progress) accumulate in .excn forever — a dozen-plus since June 6 on the dogfood Instance. Add pruning: doctor warns on aged records and/or a clerk-run chore archives or deletes records older than N closed sprints. Decide retention policy at implementation.

issues.title: Scaffolder stamps make-teammate; Preflight's global-skill gate drops to zero
issues.description: make-teammate is generic and identical across projects, so per the framework's own invariant rule it belongs in the package: the Template ships it beside the stamped Lifecycle skills and init stamps it into every Instance's .claude/skills/. The Setup Skill's Preflight stops requiring any global skill (the make-teammate gate goes; the only remaining prerequisite is a working node/npx). Personal-level copies override the stamped one on collision — benign, same content family. Removes the last external dependency blocking plugin-marketplace publication. End-to-end: a fresh adopter with no personal skills installs the plugin, runs the Setup Skill, and Preflight passes; init stamps make-teammate; the Team step of the Setup Grill works.
acceptance_criteria: src/template ships make-teammate; init stamps it into .claude/skills/
Setup Skill Preflight requires no global skills
ADR-0003 amended: make-teammate joins the stamped skill set
package-qa PASS (manifest grows by the new template file; pack parity holds)
Dogfood root mirrors the stamped copy (Src-First)
Ships in the next npm release
issues.root_cause: make-teammate predates the Scaffolder and stayed a personal global skill after the lifecycle skills were stamped (ADR-0003)
issues.notes: Surfaced 2026-06-10 scoping plugin publication, immediately after EXEC-073 removed the async-questions gate.

issues.title: Stamped hooks break under host "type":"module" — ship template hooks as .cjs
issues.description: All hooks in src/template/.claude/hooks/ are CommonJS .js files. When a host project's package.json declares "type":"module" (observed live in evo-gnit), Node treats every .js hook as ESM and each throws ReferenceError: require is not defined in ES module scope — gate-watch, spawn-guard, message-nudge, viewer-server, load-report, hook-lib, and every rules file silently or visibly fail. Rename the stamped hook and rules files to .cjs, update intra-hook require paths (e.g. require('./hook-lib') → './hook-lib.cjs'), and update every hook command in src/template/.claude/settings.json to point at the .cjs filenames. The Scaffolder must stamp the .cjs layout for new Instances.
acceptance_criteria: Every file in src/template/.claude/hooks/ uses the .cjs extension; no .js hook or rules file remains in the template.
All intra-hook require() paths resolve to the .cjs filenames.
src/template/.claude/settings.json hook commands reference the .cjs paths.
A scaffold stamped into a temp project whose package.json has "type":"module" runs every hook (PostToolUse, Stop, spawn-guard, viewer-server) without an ESM ReferenceError.
package-qa stamp-contract check passes on the new manifest.
issues.root_cause: Stamped hooks were CommonJS .js; host package.json "type":"module" made Node parse them as ESM, so require() threw.
issues.fix: Template hooks renamed to .cjs with settings.json/requires/health-policy updated; landed in commit c624700 after code-standards and package-qa PASS.

issues.title: Migrate already-stamped Instances to the .cjs hook layout
issues.description: Instances stamped before EXEC-075 (e.g. evo-gnit) carry the broken .js hooks and a settings.json pointing at them; under host "type":"module" every hook fails at require(). Extend migrate-policy so the Migrate command renames the stamped hook/rules files to .cjs, rewrites the settings.json hook commands, and removes the stale .js copies. The health check should flag an Instance still on the .js layout.
acceptance_criteria: Running the Migrate command on an Instance stamped with the old .js hooks leaves it on the .cjs layout: hooks renamed, settings.json commands updated, no stale .js hook files.
Migration is idempotent — a second run is a no-op.
Health check reports the .js layout as decayed/migration-due.
Verified end-to-end on evo-gnit: Stop hook runs without the ESM ReferenceError.
issues.root_cause: Instances stamped before EXEC-075 carry CommonJS .js hooks that break under host "type":"module".
issues.fix: migrate renames legacy .js hooks to .cjs (hash-guarded, idempotent, marker re-keying), rewrites settings.json commands and sibling requires; doctor flags .js layout MIGRATION-DUE. Landed in commit 7b54a6e after code-standards and package-qa PASS; verified live on evo-gnit.

issues.title: Sprint-close team-teardown reminder hook
issues.description: Persistent Teammates spawned for a sprint outlive sprint close, bloating context with idle-notification traffic and stale team/task dirs (~/.claude/teams, ~/.claude/tasks). Add a remind-only hook (gate-watch pattern, fail-safe exit 0) that fires when an edit closes a sprint — Write/Edit on .excn/sprints/sprint_<N>.json setting status to closed, or an equivalently clear close trigger — and injects a reminder to tear the team down: shutdown_request each Teammate, then TeamDelete. Remind-only; never tears down itself.
acceptance_criteria: A hook detects a sprint-close edit (sprint JSON status flipped to closed, or an equivalently unambiguous trigger) and injects a teardown reminder naming the steps: shutdown_request each persistent Teammate, await responses, TeamDelete.
Remind-only: the hook performs no teardown and never blocks more than once (gate-watch stop_hook_active pattern).
Fail-safe: missing/malformed config or payload exits 0 with no output.
No reminder fires on sprint edits that do not close the sprint.
Hook ships as .cjs in the template, logs invocations via hook-lib, and passes code-standards + package-qa gates.
